records.ety: [Pref. dis- + approve: cf. F. dapprouver. Cf. Disapprobation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disapproved (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disapproving.]

defs: To pass unfavorable judgment upon; to condemn by an act of the judgment; to regard as wrong, unsuitable, or inexpedient; to censure; as, to disapprove the conduct of others.
To refuse official approbation to; to disallow; to decline to sanction; as, the sentence of the court-martial was disapproved by the commander in chief.
notes: ☞ This verb is often followed by of; as, to disapprove of an opinion, of such conduct. See Approve.
